NKJV.1: Inasmuch as many have taken in hand to set in order a narrative of those things which have been fulfilled among us,
NKJV.2: just as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and ministers of the word delivered them to us,
NKJV.3: it seemed good to me also, having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first, to write to you an orderly account, most excellent Theophilus,
NKJV.4: that you may know the certainty of those things in which you were instructed.
NKJV.5: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the division of Abijah. His wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
NKJV.6: And they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.
NKJV.7: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both well advanced in years.
NKJV.8: So it was, that while he was serving as priest before God in the order of his division,
NKJV.9: according to the custom of the priesthood, his lot fell to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.
NKJV.10: And the whole multitude of the people was praying outside at the hour of incense.
NKJV.11: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
NKJV.12: And when Zacharias saw him, he was troubled, and fear fell upon him.
NKJV.13: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zacharias, for your prayer is heard; and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.
NKJV.14: And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth.
NKJV.15: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink. He will also be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother’s womb.
NKJV.16: And he will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.
NKJV.17: He will also go before Him in the spirit and power of Elijah, ‘to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children,’ and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
NKJV.18: And Zacharias said to the angel, “How shall I know this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.”
NKJV.19: And the angel answered and said to him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and was sent to speak to you and bring you these glad tidings.
NKJV.20: But behold, you will be mute and not able to speak until the day these things take place, because you did not believe my words which will be fulfilled in their own time.”
NKJV.21: And the people waited for Zacharias, and marveled that he lingered so long in the temple.
NKJV.22: But when he came out, he could not speak to them; and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple, for he beckoned to them and remained speechless.
NKJV.23: So it was, as soon as the days of his service were completed, that he departed to his own house.
NKJV.24: Now after those days his wife Elizabeth conceived; and she hid herself five months, saying,
NKJV.25: “Thus the Lord has dealt with me, in the days when He looked on me, to take away my reproach among people.”
NKJV.26: Now in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a city of Galilee named Nazareth,
NKJV.27: to a virgin betrothed to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.
NKJV.28: And having come in, the angel said to her, “Rejoice, highly favored one, the Lord is with you; blessed are you among women!”
NKJV.29: But when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying, and considered what manner of greeting this was.
NKJV.30: Then the angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
NKJV.31: And behold, you will conceive in your womb and bring forth a Son, and shall call His name JESUS.
NKJV.32: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Highest; and the Lord God will give Him the throne of His father David.
NKJV.33: And He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and of His kingdom there will be no end.”
NKJV.34: Then Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, since I do not know a man?”
NKJV.35: And the angel answered and said to her, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Highest will overshadow you; therefore, also, that Holy One who is to be born will be called the Son of God.
NKJV.36: Now indeed, Elizabeth your relative has also conceived a son in her old age; and this is now the sixth month for her who was called barren.
NKJV.37: For with God nothing will be impossible.”
NKJV.38: Then Mary said, “Behold the maidservant of the Lord! Let it be to me according to your word.” And the angel departed from her.
NKJV.39: Now Mary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste, to a city of Judah,
NKJV.40: and entered the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.
NKJV.41: And it happened, when Elizabeth heard the greeting of Mary, that the babe leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.42: Then she spoke out with a loud voice and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!
NKJV.43: But why is this granted to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
NKJV.44: For indeed, as soon as the voice of your greeting sounded in my ears, the babe leaped in my womb for joy.
NKJV.45: Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of those things which were told her from the Lord.”
NKJV.46: And Mary said:  “My soul magnifies the Lord,
NKJV.47: And my spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior.
NKJV.48: For He has regarded the lowly state of His maidservant; For behold, henceforth all generations will call me blessed.
NKJV.49: For He who is mighty has done great things for me, And holy is His name.
NKJV.50: And His mercy is on those who fear Him From generation to generation.
NKJV.51: He has shown strength with His arm; He has scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.
NKJV.52: He has put down the mighty from their thrones, And exalted the lowly.
NKJV.53: He has filled the hungry with good things, And the rich He has sent away empty.
NKJV.54: He has helped His servant Israel, In remembrance of His mercy,
NKJV.55: As He spoke to our fathers, To Abraham and to his seed forever.”
NKJV.56: And Mary remained with her about three months, and returned to her house.
NKJV.57: Now Elizabeth’s full time came for her to be delivered, and she brought forth a son.
NKJV.58: When her neighbors and relatives heard how the Lord had shown great mercy to her, they rejoiced with her.
NKJV.59: So it was, on the eighth day, that they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him by the name of his father, Zacharias.
NKJV.60: His mother answered and said, “No; he shall be called John.”
NKJV.61: But they said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who is called by this name.”
NKJV.62: So they made signs to his father—what he would have him called.
NKJV.63: And he asked for a writing tablet, and wrote, saying, “His name is John.” So they all marveled.
NKJV.64: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue loosed, and he spoke, praising God.
NKJV.65: Then fear came on all who dwelt around them; and all these sayings were discussed throughout all the hill country of Judea.
NKJV.66: And all those who heard them kept them in their hearts, saying, “What kind of child will this be?” And the hand of the Lord was with him.
NKJV.67: Now his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying:
NKJV.68: “Blessed is the Lord God of Israel, For He has visited and redeemed His people,
NKJV.69: And has raised up a horn of salvation for us In the house of His servant David,
NKJV.70: As He spoke by the mouth of His holy prophets, Who have been since the world began,
NKJV.71: That we should be saved from our enemies And from the hand of all who hate us,
NKJV.72: To perform the mercy promised to our fathers And to remember His holy covenant,
NKJV.73: The oath which He swore to our father Abraham:
NKJV.74: To grant us that we, Being delivered from the hand of our enemies, Might serve Him without fear,
NKJV.75: In holiness and righteousness before Him all the days of our life.
NKJV.76: “And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Highest; For you will go before the face of the Lord to prepare His ways,
NKJV.77: To give knowledge of salvation to His people By the remission of their sins,
NKJV.78: Through the tender mercy of our God, With which the Dayspring from on high has visited us;
NKJV.79: To give light to those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death, To guide our feet into the way of peace.”
NKJV.80: So the child grew and became strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his manifestation to Israel.

ESV.1: Inasmuch as many have undertaken to compile a narrative of the things that have been accomplished among us,
ESV.2: just as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and ministers of the word have delivered them to us,
ESV.3: it seemed good to me also, having followed all things closely for some time past, to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
ESV.4: that you may have certainty concerning the things you have been taught.
ESV.5: In the days of Herod, king of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah,  of the division of Abijah. And he had a wife from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
ESV.6: And they were both righteous before God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and statutes of the Lord.
ESV.7: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and both were advanced in years.
ESV.8: Now while he was serving as priest before God when his division was on duty,
ESV.9: according to the custom of the priesthood, he was chosen by lot to enter the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
ESV.10: And the whole multitude of the people were praying outside at the hour of incense.
ESV.11: And there appeared to him an angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
ESV.12: And Zechariah was troubled when he saw him, and fear fell upon him.
ESV.13: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.
ESV.14: And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
ESV.15: for he will be great before the Lord. And he must not drink wine or strong drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother's womb.
ESV.16: And he will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God,
ESV.17: and he will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just, to make ready for the Lord a people prepared.”
ESV.18: And Zechariah said to the angel, “How shall I know this? For I am an old man, and my wife is advanced in years.”
ESV.19: And the angel answered him, “I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I was sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
ESV.20: And behold, you will be silent and unable to speak until the day that these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time.”
ESV.21: And the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they were wondering at his delay in the temple.
ESV.22: And when he came out, he was unable to speak to them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the temple. And he kept making signs to them and remained mute.
ESV.23: And when his time of service was ended, he went to his home.
ESV.24: After these days his wife Elizabeth conceived, and for five months she kept herself hidden, saying,
ESV.25: “Thus the Lord has done for me in the days when he looked on me, to take away my reproach among people.”
ESV.26: In the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee named Nazareth,
ESV.27: to a virgin betrothed  to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. And the virgin's name was Mary.
ESV.28: And he came to her and said, “Greetings, O favored one, the Lord is with you!”
ESV.29: But she was greatly troubled at the saying, and tried to discern what sort of greeting this might be.
ESV.30: And the angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
ESV.31: And behold, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus.
ESV.32: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. And the Lord God will give to him the throne of his father David,
ESV.33: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and of his kingdom there will be no end.”
ESV.34: And Mary said to the angel, “How will this be, since I am a virgin?”
ESV.35: And the angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore the child to be born  will be called holy — the Son of God.
ESV.36: And behold, your relative Elizabeth in her old age has also conceived a son, and this is the sixth month with her who was called barren.
ESV.37: For nothing will be impossible with God.”
ESV.38: And Mary said, “Behold, I am the servant  of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word.” And the angel departed from her.
ESV.39: In those days Mary arose and went with haste into the hill country, to a town in Judah,
ESV.40: and she entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.
ESV.41: And when Elizabeth heard the greeting of Mary, the baby leaped in her womb. And Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit,
ESV.42: and she exclaimed with a loud cry, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!
ESV.43: And why is this granted to me that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
ESV.44: For behold, when the sound of your greeting came to my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.
ESV.45: And blessed is she who believed that there would be  a fulfillment of what was spoken to her from the Lord.”
ESV.46: And Mary said, “My soul magnifies the Lord,
ESV.47: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
ESV.48: for he has looked on the humble estate of his servant. For behold, from now on all generations will call me blessed;
ESV.49: for he who is mighty has done great things for me, and holy is his name.
ESV.50: And his mercy is for those who fear him from generation to generation.
ESV.51: He has shown strength with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts;
ESV.52: he has brought down the mighty from their thrones and exalted those of humble estate;
ESV.53: he has filled the hungry with good things, and the rich he has sent away empty.
ESV.54: He has helped his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy,
ESV.55: as he spoke to our fathers, to Abraham and to his offspring forever.”
ESV.56: And Mary remained with her about three months and returned to her home.
ESV.57: Now the time came for Elizabeth to give birth, and she bore a son.
ESV.58: And her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown great mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.
ESV.59: And on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child. And they would have called him Zechariah after his father,
ESV.60: but his mother answered, “No; he shall be called John.”
ESV.61: And they said to her, “None of your relatives is called by this name.”
ESV.62: And they made signs to his father, inquiring what he wanted him to be called.
ESV.63: And he asked for a writing tablet and wrote, “His name is John.” And they all wondered.
ESV.64: And immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue loosed, and he spoke, blessing God.
ESV.65: And fear came on all their neighbors. And all these things were talked about through all the hill country of Judea,
ESV.66: and all who heard them laid them up in their hearts, saying, “What then will this child be?” For the hand of the Lord was with him.
ESV.67: And his father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied, saying,
ESV.68: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he has visited and redeemed his people
ESV.69: and has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David,
ESV.70: as he spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets from of old,
ESV.71: that we should be saved from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us;
ESV.72: to show the mercy promised to our fathers and to remember his holy covenant,
ESV.73: the oath that he swore to our father Abraham, to grant us
ESV.74: that we, being delivered from the hand of our enemies, might serve him without fear,
ESV.75: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
ESV.76: And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High; for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
ESV.77: to give knowledge of salvation to his people in the forgiveness of their sins,
ESV.78: because of the tender mercy of our God, whereby the sunrise shall visit us  from on high
ESV.79: to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.”
ESV.80: And the child grew and became strong in spirit, and he was in the wilderness until the day of his public appearance to Israel.

NASB.1: Inasmuch as many have undertaken to compile an account of the things accomplished among us,
NASB.2: just as they were handed down to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word,
NASB.3: it seemed fitting for me as well, having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, to write [it] out for you in consecutive order, most excellent Theophilus;
NASB.4: so that you may know the exact truth about the things you have been taught.
NASB.5: In the days of Herod, king of Judea, there was a priest named Zacharias, of the division of Abijah; and he had a wife from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
NASB.6: They were both righteous in the sight of God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and requirements of the Lord.
NASB.7: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both advanced in years.
NASB.8: Now it happened [that] while he was performing his priestly service before God in the [appointed] order of his division,
NASB.9: according to the custom of the priestly office, he was chosen by lot to enter the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
NASB.10: And the whole multitude of the people were in prayer outside at the hour of the incense offering.
NASB.11: And an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
NASB.12: Zacharias was troubled when he saw [the angel], and fear gripped him.
NASB.13: But the angel said to him, 'Do not be afraid, Zacharias, for your petition has been heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will give him the name John.
NASB.14: 'You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth.
NASB.15: 'For he will be great in the sight of the Lord; and he will drink no wine or liquor, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit while yet in his mother's womb.
NASB.16: 'And he will turn many of the sons of Israel back to the Lord their God.
NASB.17: 'It is he who will go [as a forerunner] before Him in the spirit and power of Elijah, TO TURN THE HEARTS OF THE FATHERS BACK TO THE CHILDREN, and the disobedient to the attitude of the righteous, so as to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.'
NASB.18: Zacharias said to the angel, 'How will I know this [for certain]? For I am an old man and my wife is advanced in years.'
NASB.19: The angel answered and said to him, 'I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
NASB.20: 'And behold, you shall be silent and unable to speak until the day when these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.'
NASB.21: The people were waiting for Zacharias, and were wondering at his delay in the temple.
NASB.22: But when he came out, he was unable to speak to them; and they realized that he had seen a vision in the temple; and he kept making signs to them, and remained mute.
NASB.23: When the days of his priestly service were ended, he went back home.
NASB.24: After these days Elizabeth his wife became pregnant, and she kept herself in seclusion for five months, saying,
NASB.25: 'This is the way the Lord has dealt with me in the days when He looked [with favor] upon [me], to take away my disgrace among men.'
NASB.26: Now in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city in Galilee called Nazareth,
NASB.27: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, of the descendants of David; and the virgin's name was Mary.
NASB.28: And coming in, he said to her, 'Greetings, favored one! The Lord [is] with you.'
NASB.29: But she was very perplexed at [this] statement, and kept pondering what kind of salutation this was.
NASB.30: The angel said to her, 'Do not be afraid, Mary; for you have found favor with God.
NASB.31: 'And behold, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you shall name Him Jesus.
NASB.32: 'He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High; and the Lord God will give Him the throne of His father David;
NASB.33: and He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and His kingdom will have no end.'
NASB.34: Mary said to the angel, 'How can this be, since I am a virgin?'
NASB.35: The angel answered and said to her, 'The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; and for that reason the holy Child shall be called the Son of God.
NASB.36: 'And behold, even your relative Elizabeth has also conceived a son in her old age; and she who was called barren is now in her sixth month.
NASB.37: 'For nothing will be impossible with God.'
NASB.38: And Mary said, 'Behold, the bondslave of the Lord; may it be done to me according to your word.' And the angel departed from her.
NASB.39: Now at this time Mary arose and went in a hurry to the hill country, to a city of Judah,
NASB.40: and entered the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.
NASB.41: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
NASB.42: And she cried out with a loud voice and said, 'Blessed [are] you among women, and blessed [is] the fruit of your womb!
NASB.43: 'And how has it [happened] to me, that the mother of my Lord would come to me?
NASB.44: 'For behold, when the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy.
NASB.45: 'And blessed [is] she who believed that there would be a fulfillment of what had been spoken to her by the Lord.'
NASB.46: And Mary said: 'My soul exalts the Lord,
NASB.47: And my spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior.
NASB.48: 'For He has had regard for the humble state of His bondslave; For behold, from this time on all generations will count me blessed.
NASB.49: 'For the Mighty One has done great things for me; And holy is His name.
NASB.50: 'AND HIS MERCY IS UPON GENERATION AFTER GENERATION TOWARD THOSE WHO FEAR HIM.
NASB.51: 'He has done mighty deeds with His arm; He has scattered [those who were] proud in the thoughts of their heart.
NASB.52: 'He has brought down rulers from [their] thrones, And has exalted those who were humble.
NASB.53: 'HE HAS FILLED THE HUNGRY WITH GOOD THINGS; And sent away the rich empty-handed.
NASB.54: 'He has given help to Israel His servant, In remembrance of His mercy,
NASB.55: As He spoke to our fathers, To Abraham and his descendants forever.'
NASB.56: And Mary stayed with her about three months, and [then] returned to her home.
NASB.57: Now the time had come for Elizabeth to give birth, and she gave birth to a son.
NASB.58: Her neighbors and her relatives heard that the Lord had displayed His great mercy toward her; and they were rejoicing with her.
NASB.59: And it happened that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to call him Zacharias, after his father.
NASB.60: But his mother answered and said, 'No indeed; but he shall be called John.'
NASB.61: And they said to her, 'There is no one among your relatives who is called by that name.'
NASB.62: And they made signs to his father, as to what he wanted him called.
NASB.63: And he asked for a tablet and wrote as follows, 'His name is John.' And they were all astonished.
NASB.64: And at once his mouth was opened and his tongue [loosed], and he [began] to speak in praise of God.
NASB.65: Fear came on all those living around them; and all these matters were being talked about in all the hill country of Judea.
NASB.66: All who heard them kept them in mind, saying, 'What then will this child [turn out to] be?' For the hand of the Lord was certainly with him.
NASB.67: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying:
NASB.68: 'Blessed [be] the Lord God of Israel, For He has visited us and accomplished redemption for His people,
NASB.69: And has raised up a horn of salvation for us In the house of David His servant--
NASB.70: As He spoke by the mouth of His holy prophets from of old--
NASB.71: Salvation FROM OUR ENEMIES, And FROM THE HAND OF ALL WHO HATE US;
NASB.72: To show mercy toward our fathers, And to remember His holy covenant,
NASB.73: The oath which He swore to Abraham our father,
NASB.74: To grant us that we, being rescued from the hand of our enemies, Might serve Him without fear,
NASB.75: In holiness and righteousness before Him all our days.
NASB.76: 'And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High; For you will go on BEFORE THE LORD TO PREPARE HIS WAYS;
NASB.77: To give to His people [the] knowledge of salvation By the forgiveness of their sins,
NASB.78: Because of the tender mercy of our God, With which the Sunrise from on high will visit us,
NASB.79: TO SHINE UPON THOSE WHO SIT IN DARKNESS AND THE SHADOW OF DEATH, To guide our feet into the way of peace.'
NASB.80: And the child continued to grow and to become strong in spirit, and he lived in the deserts until the day of his public appearance to Israel.

NLT.1: Many people have set out to write accounts about the events that have been fulfilled among us.
NLT.2: They used the eyewitness reports circulating among us from the early disciples.
NLT.3: Having carefully investigated everything from the beginning, I also have decided to write an accurate account for you, most honorable Theophilus,
NLT.4: so you can be certain of the truth of everything you were taught.
NLT.5: When Herod was king of Judea, there was a Jewish priest named Zechariah. He was a member of the priestly order of Abijah, and his wife, Elizabeth, was also from the priestly line of Aaron.
NLT.6: Zechariah and Elizabeth were righteous in God’s eyes, careful to obey all of the Lord’s commandments and regulations.
NLT.7: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to conceive, and they were both very old.
NLT.8: One day Zechariah was serving God in the Temple, for his order was on duty that week.
NLT.9: As was the custom of the priests, he was chosen by lot to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and burn incense.
NLT.10: While the incense was being burned, a great crowd stood outside, praying.
NLT.11: While Zechariah was in the sanctuary, an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the incense altar.
NLT.12: Zechariah was shaken and overwhelmed with fear when he saw him.
NLT.13: But the angel said, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife, Elizabeth, will give you a son, and you are to name him John.
NLT.14: You will have great joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
NLT.15: for he will be great in the eyes of the Lord. He must never touch wine or other alcoholic drinks. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth.
NLT.16: And he will turn many Israelites to the Lord their God.
NLT.17: He will be a man with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will prepare the people for the coming of the Lord. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and he will cause those who are rebellious to accept the wisdom of the godly.”
NLT.18: Zechariah said to the angel, “How can I be sure this will happen? I’m an old man now, and my wife is also well along in years.”
NLT.19: Then the angel said, “I am Gabriel! I stand in the very presence of God. It was he who sent me to bring you this good news!
NLT.20: But now, since you didn’t believe what I said, you will be silent and unable to speak until the child is born. For my words will certainly be fulfilled at the proper time.”
NLT.21: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah to come out of the sanctuary, wondering why he was taking so long.
NLT.22: When he finally did come out, he couldn’t speak to them. Then they realized from his gestures and his silence that he must have seen a vision in the sanctuary.
NLT.23: When Zechariah’s week of service in the Temple was over, he returned home.
NLT.24: Soon afterward his wife, Elizabeth, became pregnant and went into seclusion for five months.
NLT.25: “How kind the Lord is!” she exclaimed. “He has taken away my disgrace of having no children.”
NLT.26: In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a village in Galilee,
NLT.27: to a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of King David.
NLT.28: Gabriel appeared to her and said, “Greetings, favored woman! The Lord is with you! ”
NLT.29: Confused and disturbed, Mary tried to think what the angel could mean.
NLT.30: “Don’t be afraid, Mary,” the angel told her, “for you have found favor with God!
NLT.31: You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
NLT.32: He will be very great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his ancestor David.
NLT.33: And he will reign over Israel forever; his Kingdom will never end!”
NLT.34: Mary asked the angel, “But how can this happen? I am a virgin.”
NLT.35: The angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the baby to be born will be holy, and he will be called the Son of God.
NLT.36: What’s more, your relative Elizabeth has become pregnant in her old age! People used to say she was barren, but she has conceived a son and is now in her sixth month.
NLT.37: For the word of God will never fail. ”
NLT.38: Mary responded, “I am the Lord’s servant. May everything you have said about me come true.” And then the angel left her.
NLT.39: A few days later Mary hurried to the hill country of Judea, to the town
NLT.40: where Zechariah lived. She entered the house and greeted Elizabeth.
NLT.41: At the sound of Mary’s greeting, Elizabeth’s child leaped within her, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
NLT.42: Elizabeth gave a glad cry and exclaimed to Mary, “God has blessed you above all women, and your child is blessed.
NLT.43: Why am I so honored, that the mother of my Lord should visit me?
NLT.44: When I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
NLT.45: You are blessed because you believed that the Lord would do what he said.”
NLT.46: Mary responded, “Oh, how my soul praises the Lord.
NLT.47: How my spirit rejoices in God my Savior!
NLT.48:
For he took notice of his lowly servant girl,
and from now on all generations will call me blessed.
NLT.49:
For the Mighty One is holy,
and he has done great things for me.
NLT.50:
He shows mercy from generation to generation
to all who fear him.
NLT.51:
His mighty arm has done tremendous things!
He has scattered the proud and haughty ones.
NLT.52:
He has brought down princes from their thrones
and exalted the humble.
NLT.53:
He has filled the hungry with good things
and sent the rich away with empty hands.
NLT.54:
He has helped his servant Israel
and remembered to be merciful.
NLT.55:
For he made this promise to our ancestors,
to Abraham and his children forever.”
NLT.56: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months and then went back to her own home.
NLT.57: When it was time for Elizabeth’s baby to be born, she gave birth to a son.
NLT.58: And when her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had been very merciful to her, everyone rejoiced with her.
NLT.59: When the baby was eight days old, they all came for the circumcision ceremony. They wanted to name him Zechariah, after his father.
NLT.60: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John!”
NLT.61: “What?” they exclaimed. “There is no one in all your family by that name.”
NLT.62: So they used gestures to ask the baby’s father what he wanted to name him.
NLT.63: He motioned for a writing tablet, and to everyone’s surprise he wrote, “His name is John.”
NLT.64: Instantly Zechariah could speak again, and he began praising God.
NLT.65: Awe fell upon the whole neighborhood, and the news of what had happened spread throughout the Judean hills.
NLT.66: Everyone who heard about it reflected on these events and asked, “What will this child turn out to be?” For the hand of the Lord was surely upon him in a special way.
NLT.67: Then his father, Zechariah, was filled with the Holy Spirit and gave this prophecy:
NLT.68:
“Praise the Lord, the God of Israel,
because he has visited and redeemed his people.
NLT.69:
He has sent us a mighty Savior
from the royal line of his servant David,
NLT.70:
just as he promised
through his holy prophets long ago.
NLT.71:
Now we will be saved from our enemies
and from all who hate us.
NLT.72:
He has been merciful to our ancestors
by remembering his sacred covenant —
NLT.73:
the covenant he swore with an oath
to our ancestor Abraham.
NLT.74:
We have been rescued from our enemies
so we can serve God without fear,
NLT.75:
in holiness and righteousness
for as long as we live.
NLT.76:
“And you, my little son,
will be called the prophet of the Most High,
because you will prepare the way for the Lord.
NLT.77:
You will tell his people how to find salvation
through forgiveness of their sins.
NLT.78:
Because of God’s tender mercy,
the morning light from heaven is about to break upon us,
NLT.79:
to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death,
and to guide us to the path of peace.”
NLT.80: John grew up and became strong in spirit. And he lived in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.

WEB.1: Since many have undertaken to set in order a narrative concerning those matters which have been fulfilled among us,
WEB.2: even as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word delivered them to us,
WEB.3: it seemed good to me also, having traced the course of all things accurately from the first, to write to you in order, most excellent Theophilus;
WEB.4: that you might know the certainty concerning the things in which you were instructed.
WEB.5: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the priestly division of Abijah. He had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
WEB.6: They were both righteous before God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord.
WEB.7: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they both were well advanced in years.
WEB.8: Now while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his division,
WEB.9: according to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to enter into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
WEB.10: The whole multitude of the people were praying outside at the hour of incense.
WEB.11: An angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
WEB.12: Zacharias was troubled when he saw him, and fear fell upon him.
WEB.13: But the angel said to him, “Don’t be afraid, Zacharias, because your request has been heard, and your wife, Elizabeth, will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.
WEB.14: You will have joy and gladness; and many will rejoice at his birth.
WEB.15: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and he will drink no wine nor strong drink. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother’s womb.
WEB.16: He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord, their God.
WEB.17: He will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, ‘to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children,’ and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to prepare a people prepared for the Lord.”
WEB.18: Zacharias said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.”
WEB.19: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God. I was sent to speak to you, and to bring you this good news.
WEB.20: Behold,  you will be silent and not able to speak, until the day that these things will happen, because you didn’t believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”
WEB.21: The people were waiting for Zacharias, and they marveled that he delayed in the temple.
WEB.22: When he came out, he could not speak to them, and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple. He continued making signs to them, and remained mute.
WEB.23: When the days of his service were fulfilled, he departed to his house.
WEB.24: After these days Elizabeth, his wife, conceived, and she hid herself five months, saying,
WEB.25: “Thus has the Lord done to me in the days in which he looked at me, to take away my reproach among men.”
WEB.26: Now in the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,
WEB.27: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man whose name was Joseph, of David’s house. The virgin’s name was Mary.
WEB.28: Having come in, the angel said to her, “Rejoice, you highly favored one! The Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women!”
WEB.29: But when she saw him, she was greatly troubled at the saying, and considered what kind of salutation this might be.
WEB.30: The angel said to her, “Don’t be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
WEB.31: Behold, you will conceive in your womb, and give birth to a son, and will call his name ‘Jesus.’
WEB.32: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father, David,
WEB.33: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever. There will be no end to his Kingdom.”
WEB.34: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, seeing I am a virgin?”
WEB.35: The angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore also the holy one who is born from you will be called the Son of God.
WEB.36: Behold, Elizabeth, your relative, also has conceived a son in her old age; and this is the sixth month with her who was called barren.
WEB.37: For nothing spoken by God is impossible.”
WEB.38: Mary said, “Behold, the servant of the Lord; let it be done to me according to your word.” The angel departed from her.
WEB.39: Mary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Judah,
WEB.40: and entered into the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.
WEB.41: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
WEB.42: She called out with a loud voice, and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!
WEB.43: Why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
WEB.44: For behold, when the voice of your greeting came into my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy!
WEB.45: Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of the things which have been spoken to her from the Lord!”
WEB.46: Mary said, “My soul magnifies the Lord.
WEB.47: My spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior,
WEB.48: for he has looked at the humble state of his servant. For behold, from now on, all generations will call me blessed.
WEB.49: For he who is mighty has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
WEB.50: His mercy is for generations of generations on those who fear him.
WEB.51: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.
WEB.52: He has put down princes from their thrones. And has exalted the lowly.
WEB.53: He has filled the hungry with good things. He has sent the rich away empty.
WEB.54: He has given help to Israel, his servant, that he might remember mercy,
WEB.55: As he spoke to our fathers, to Abraham and his offspring  forever.”
WEB.56: Mary stayed with her about three months, and then returned to her house.
WEB.57: Now the time that Elizabeth should give birth was fulfilled, and she gave birth to a son.
WEB.58: Her neighbors and her relatives heard that the Lord had magnified his mercy towards her, and they rejoiced with her.
WEB.59: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.
WEB.60: His mother answered, “Not so; but he will be called John.”
WEB.61: They said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who is called by this name.”
WEB.62: They made signs to his father, what he would have him called.
WEB.63: He asked for a writing tablet, and wrote, “His name is John.” They all marveled.
WEB.64: His mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue freed, and he spoke, blessing God.
WEB.65: Fear came on all who lived around them, and all these sayings were talked about throughout all the hill country of Judea.
WEB.66: All who heard them laid them up in their heart, saying, “What then will this child be?” The hand of the Lord was with him.
WEB.67: His father, Zacharias, was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying,
WEB.68: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, for he has visited and redeemed his people;
WEB.69: and has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David
WEB.70: (as he spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets who have been from of old),
WEB.71: salvation from our enemies, and from the hand of all who hate us;
WEB.72: to show mercy towards our fathers, to remember his holy covenant,
WEB.73: the oath which he swore to Abraham, our father,
WEB.74: to grant to us that we, being delivered out of the hand of our enemies, should serve him without fear,
WEB.75: In holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our life.
WEB.76: And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways,
WEB.77: to give knowledge of salvation to his people by the remission of their sins,
WEB.78: because of the tender mercy of our God, whereby the dawn from on high will visit us,
WEB.79: to shine on those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death; to guide our feet into the way of peace.”
WEB.80: The child was growing, and becoming strong in spirit, and was in the desert until the day of his public appearance to Israel.

CSB17.1: Many have undertaken to compile a narrative about the events that have been fulfilled <sup>[1]</sup> among us, <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.2: just as the original eyewitnesses <sup>ⓑ</sup> and servants of the word <sup>ⓒ</sup> handed them down to us.
CSB17.3: It also seemed good to me, since I have carefully investigated everything from the very first, to write to you in an orderly sequence, most honorable <sup>ⓓ</sup> Theophilus, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.4: so that you may know the certainty of the things about which you have been instructed. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.5: In the days of King Herod <sup>ⓖ</sup> of Judea, there was a priest of Abijah’s division <sup>ⓗ</sup> named Zechariah. His wife was from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
CSB17.6: Both were righteous in God’s sight, <sup>ⓘ</sup> living without blame <sup>ⓙ</sup> according to all the commands and requirements of the Lord.
CSB17.7: But they had no children because Elizabeth could not conceive, and both of them were well along in years. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.8: When his division was on duty <sup>ⓛ</sup> and he was serving as priest before God,
CSB17.9: it happened that he was chosen by lot, <sup>ⓜ</sup> according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and burn incense. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.10: At the hour of incense the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.
CSB17.11: An angel of the Lord <sup>ⓐ</sup> appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.12: When Zechariah saw him, he was terrified and overcome with fear. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.13: But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid, <sup>ⓓ</sup> Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard. <sup>ⓔ</sup> Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.14: There will be joy and delight for you, and many will rejoice at his birth.
CSB17.15: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord and will never drink wine or beer. <sup>ⓖ</sup> He will be filled with the Holy Spirit <sup>ⓗ</sup> while still in his mother’s womb. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.16: He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.17: And he will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of fathers to their children, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and the disobedient to the understanding of the righteous, to make ready for the Lord a prepared people.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.18: “How can I know this?” Zechariah asked the angel. “For I am an old man, and my wife is well along in years.”
CSB17.19: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, <sup>ⓜ</sup> who stands in the presence of God, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and I was sent to speak to you and tell you this good news.
CSB17.20: Now listen. You will become silent and unable to speak until the day these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”
CSB17.21: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, amazed that he stayed so long in the sanctuary.
CSB17.22: When he did come out, he could not speak to them. Then they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He was making signs to them <sup>ⓐ</sup> and remained speechless.
CSB17.23: When the days of his ministry were completed, he went back home.
CSB17.24: After these days his wife Elizabeth conceived and kept herself in seclusion for five months. She said,
CSB17.25: “The Lord has done this for me. He has looked with favor in these days to take away my disgrace <sup>ⓑ</sup> among the people.”
CSB17.26: In the sixth month, the angel Gabriel <sup>ⓒ</sup> was sent by God to a town in Galilee called Nazareth, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.27: to a virgin engaged <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> to a man named Joseph, of the house of David. <sup>ⓕ</sup> The virgin’s name was Mary.
CSB17.28: And the angel came to her and said, “Greetings, favored woman! The Lord is with you.” <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.29: But she was deeply troubled <sup>ⓖ</sup> by this statement, wondering what kind of greeting this could be.

AMP.1: Since [as is well known] many have undertaken to compile an orderly account of the things which have been fulfilled among us [by God],
AMP.2: exactly as they were handed down to us by those [with personal experience] who from the beginning [of Christ's ministry] were eyewitnesses and ministers of the word [that is, of the teaching concerning salvation through faith in Christ],
AMP.3: it seemed fitting for me as well, [and so I have decided] after having carefully searched out and investigated all the events accurately, from the very beginning, to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus;
AMP.4: so that you may know the exact truth about the things you have been taught [that is, the history and doctrine of the faith].
AMP.5: In the days of Herod [the Great], king of Judea, there was a certain priest whose name was Zacharias, of the division of Abijah. His wife was a descendant of Aaron [the first high priest of Israel], and her name was Elizabeth.
AMP.6: They both were righteous (approved) in the sight of God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and requirements of the Lord.
AMP.7: But they were childless, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both far advanced in years.
AMP.8: Now it happened while Zacharias was serving as priest before God in the appointed order of his priestly division,
AMP.9: as was the custom of the priesthood, he was chosen by lot to enter [the sanctuary of] the temple of the Lord and burn incense [on the altar of incense].
AMP.10: And all the congregation was praying outside [in the court of the temple] at the hour of the incense offering.
AMP.11: And an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
AMP.12: When Zacharias saw the angel, he was troubled and overcome with fear.
AMP.13: But the angel said to him, "Do not be afraid, Zacharias, because your petition [in prayer] was heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John.
AMP.14: You will have great joy and delight, and many will rejoice over his birth,
AMP.15: for he will be great and distinguished in the sight of the Lord; and will never drink wine or liquor, and he will be filled with and empowered to act by the Holy Spirit while still in his mother's womb.
AMP.16: He will turn many of the sons of Israel back [from sin] to [love and serve] the Lord their God.
AMP.17: It is he who will go as a forerunner before Him in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers back to the children, and the disobedient to the attitude of the righteous [which is to seek and submit to the will of God]—in order to make ready a people [perfectly] prepared [spiritually and morally] for the Lord."
AMP.18: And Zacharias said to the angel, "How will I be certain of this? For I am an old man and my wife is advanced in age."
AMP.19: The angel replied and said to him, "I am Gabriel; I stand and minister in the [very] presence of God, and I have been sent [by Him] to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
AMP.20: Listen carefully, you will be continually silent and unable to speak until the day when these things take place, because you did not believe what I told you; but my words will be fulfilled at their proper time."
AMP.21: The people [outside in the court] were waiting for Zacharias, and were wondering about his long delay in the temple.
AMP.22: But when he did come out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized that he had seen a vision in the temple; and he kept making signs to them, and remained mute.
AMP.23: When his time of priestly service was finished, he returned to his home.
AMP.24: Now after this his wife Elizabeth became pregnant, and for five months she secluded herself completely, saying,
AMP.25: "This is how the Lord has dealt with me in the days when He looked with favor on me, to take away my disgrace among men."
AMP.26: Now in the sixth month [of Elizabeth's pregnancy] the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city in Galilee called Nazareth,
AMP.27: to a virgin betrothed to a man whose name was Joseph, a descendant of the house of David; and the virgin's name was Mary.
AMP.28: And coming to her, the angel said, "Greetings, favored one! The Lord is with you."
AMP.29: But she was greatly perplexed at what he said, and kept carefully considering what kind of greeting this was.
AMP.30: The angel said to her, "Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
AMP.31: Listen carefully: you will conceive in your womb and give birth to a son, and you shall name Him Jesus.
AMP.32: He will be great and eminent and will be called the Son of the Most High; and the Lord God will give Him the throne of His father David;
AMP.33: and He will reign over the house of Jacob (Israel) forever, and of His kingdom there shall be no end."
AMP.34: Mary said to the angel, "How will this be, since I am a virgin and have no intimacy with any man?"
AMP.35: Then the angel replied to her, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you [like a cloud]; for that reason the holy (pure, sinless) Child shall be called the Son of God.
AMP.36: And listen, even your relative Elizabeth has also conceived a son in her old age; and she who was called barren is now in her sixth month.
AMP.37: For with God nothing [is or ever] shall be impossible."
AMP.38: Then Mary said, " Behold, I am the servant of the Lord; may it be done to me according to your word." And the angel left her.
AMP.39: Now at this time Mary arose and hurried to the hill country, to a city of Judah (Judea),
AMP.40: and she entered the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.
AMP.41: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit and empowered by Him.
AMP.42: And she exclaimed loudly, "Blessed [worthy to be praised] are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!
AMP.43: And how has it happened to me, that the mother of my Lord would come to me?
AMP.44: For behold, when the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.
AMP.45: And blessed [spiritually fortunate and favored by God] is she who believed and confidently trusted that there would be a fulfillment of the things that were spoken to her [by the angel sent] from the Lord."
AMP.46:
And Mary said,
"My soul magnifies and exalts the Lord,
AMP.47: And my spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior.
AMP.48:
"For He has looked [with loving care] on the humble state of His maidservant;
For behold, from now on all generations will count me blessed and happy and favored by God!
AMP.49:
"For He who is mighty has done great things for me;
And holy is His name [to be worshiped in His purity, majesty, and glory].
AMP.50:
"And His mercy is upon generation after generation
Toward those who [stand in great awe of God and] fear Him.
from generation to generation.
AMP.51:
"He has done mighty deeds with His [powerful] arm;
He has scattered those who were proud in the thoughts of their heart.
AMP.52:
"He has brought down rulers from their thrones,
And exalted those who were humble.
AMP.53:
"He has filled the hungry with good things;
And sent the rich away empty-handed.
AMP.54:
"He has helped His servant Israel,
In remembrance of His mercy,
AMP.55:
Just as He promised to our fathers,
To Abraham and to his descendants forever."
AMP.56: And Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months, and then returned to her home.
AMP.57: Now the time had come for Elizabeth to give birth, and she gave birth to a son.
AMP.58: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown His great mercy toward her, and they were rejoicing with her.
AMP.59: It happened that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child [as required by the Law], and they intended to name him Zacharias, after his father;
AMP.60: but his mother answered, "No indeed; instead he will be called John."
AMP.61: And they said to her, "None of your relatives is called by that name."
AMP.62: Then they made signs to his father, as to what he wanted him called.
AMP.63: And he asked for a writing tablet and wrote as follows, "His name is John." And they were all astonished.
AMP.64: At once Zacharias' mouth was opened and his tongue freed, and he began speaking, praising and blessing and thanking God.
AMP.65: Then fear came on all their neighbors; and all these things were discussed throughout the hill country of Judea.
AMP.66: All who heard these things kept them in mind, saying, "What then will this little boy turn out to be?" For the hand of the Lord was certainly with him [to bring about his birth].
AMP.67: Now Zacharias his father was filled with the Holy Spirit and empowered by Him, and he prophesied, saying,
AMP.68:
"Blessed (praised, glorified) be the Lord, the God of Israel,
Because He has visited us and brought redemption to His people,
AMP.69:
And He has raised up a horn of salvation [a mighty and valiant Savior] for us
In the house of David His servant—
AMP.70: Just as He promised by the mouth of His holy prophets from the most ancient times—
AMP.71:
Salvation from our enemies,
And from the hand of all who hate us;
AMP.72:
To show mercy [as He promised] to our fathers,
And to remember His holy covenant [the promised blessing],
AMP.73: The oath which He swore to Abraham our father,
AMP.74:
To grant us that we, being rescued from the hand of our enemies,
Might serve Him without fear,
AMP.75: In holiness [being set apart] and righteousness [being upright] before Him all our days.
AMP.76:
"And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High;
For you will go on before the Lord (the Messiah) to prepare His ways;
AMP.77:
To give His people the knowledge of salvation
By the forgiveness of their sins,
AMP.78:
Because of the tender mercy of our God,
With which the Sunrise (the Messiah) from on high will dawn and visit us,
AMP.79:
To shine upon those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death,
To guide our feet [in a straight line] into the way of peace and serenity."
AMP.80: The child continued to grow and to become strong in spirit, and he lived in the deserts until the day of his public appearance to Israel [as John the Baptist, the forerunner of the Messiah].

MSG.1: So many others have tried their hand at putting together a story of the wonderful harvest of Scripture and history that took place among us,
MSG.2: using reports handed down by the original eyewitnesses who served this Word with their very lives.
MSG.3: Since I have investigated all the reports in close detail, starting from the story's beginning, I decided to write it all out for you, most honorable Theophilus,
MSG.4: so you can know beyond the shadow of a doubt the reliability of what you were taught.
MSG.5: During the rule of Herod, King of Judea, there was a priest assigned service in the regiment of Abijah. His name was Zachariah. His wife was descended from the daughters of Aaron. Her name was Elizabeth.
MSG.6: Together they lived honorably before God, careful in keeping to the ways of the commandments and enjoying a clear conscience before God.
MSG.7: But they were childless because Elizabeth could never conceive, and now they were quite old.
MSG.8: It so happened that as Zachariah was carrying out his priestly duties before God, working the shift assigned to his regiment,
MSG.9: it came his one turn in life to enter the sanctuary of God and burn incense.
MSG.10: The congregation was gathered and praying outside the Temple at the hour of the incense offering.
MSG.11: Unannounced, an angel of God appeared just to the right of the altar of incense.
MSG.13: But the angel reassured him, "Don't fear, Zachariah. Your prayer has been heard. Elizabeth, your wife, will bear a son by you. You are to name him John.
MSG.14: You're going to leap like a gazelle for joy, and not only you—many will delight in his birth.
MSG.15: He'll achieve great stature with God. "He'll drink neither wine nor beer. He'll be filled with the Holy Spirit from the moment he leaves his mother's womb.
MSG.16: He will turn many sons and daughters of Israel back to their God.
MSG.17: He will herald God's arrival in the style and strength of Elijah, soften the hearts of parents to children, and kindle devout understanding among hardened skeptics—he'll get the people ready for God."
MSG.18: Zachariah said to the angel, "Do you expect me to believe this? I'm an old man and my wife is an old woman."
MSG.19: But the angel said, "I am Gabriel, the sentinel of God, sent especially to bring you this glad news.
MSG.20: But because you won't believe me, you'll be unable to say a word until the day of your son's birth. Every word I've spoken to you will come true on time—God's time."
MSG.21: Meanwhile, the congregation waiting for Zachariah was getting restless, wondering what was keeping him so long in the sanctuary.
MSG.22: When he came out and couldn't speak, they knew he had seen a vision. He continued speechless and had to use sign language with the people.
MSG.23: When the course of his priestly assignment was completed, he went back home.
MSG.24: It wasn't long before his wife, Elizabeth, conceived. She went off by herself for five months, relishing her pregnancy.
MSG.25: "So, this is how God acts to remedy my unfortunate condition!" she said.
MSG.26: In the sixth month of Elizabeth's pregnancy, God sent the angel Gabriel to the Galilean village of Nazareth
MSG.27: to a virgin engaged to be married to a man descended from David. His name was Joseph, and the virgin's name, Mary.
MSG.28: Upon entering, Gabriel greeted her: Good morning! You're beautiful with God's beauty, Beautiful inside and out! God be with you.
MSG.29: She was thoroughly shaken, wondering what was behind a greeting like that.
MSG.30: But the angel assured her, "Mary, you have nothing to fear. God has a surprise for you:
MSG.31: You will become pregnant and give birth to a son and call his name Jesus.
MSG.32: He will be great, be called 'Son of the Highest.' The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David;
MSG.33: He will rule Jacob's house forever— no end, ever, to his kingdom."
MSG.34: Mary said to the angel, "But how? I've never slept with a man."
MSG.35: The angel answered, The Holy Spirit will come upon you, the power of the Highest hover over you; Therefore, the child you bring to birth will be called Holy, Son of God.
MSG.36: "And did you know that your cousin Elizabeth conceived a son, old as she is? Everyone called her barren, and here she is six months' pregnant!
MSG.37: Nothing, you see, is impossible with God."
MSG.38: And Mary said, Yes, I see it all now: I'm the Lord's maid, ready to serve. Let it be with me just as you say. Then the angel left her.
MSG.39: Mary didn't waste a minute. She got up and traveled to a town in Judah in the hill country,
MSG.40: straight to Zachariah's house, and greeted Elizabeth.
MSG.41: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby in her womb leaped. She was filled with the Holy Spirit,
MSG.42: and sang out exuberantly, You're so blessed among women, and the babe in your womb, also blessed!
MSG.43: And why am I so blessed that the mother of my Lord visits me?
MSG.44: The moment the sound of your greeting entered my ears, The babe in my womb skipped like a lamb for sheer joy.
MSG.45: Blessed woman, who believed what God said, believed every word would come true!
MSG.46: And Mary said, I'm bursting with God-news;
MSG.47: I'm dancing the song of my Savior God.
MSG.48: God took one good look at me, and look what happened— I'm the most fortunate woman on earth! What God has done for me will never be forgotten,
MSG.49: the God whose very name is holy, set apart from all others.
MSG.50: His mercy flows in wave after wave on those who are in awe before him.
MSG.51: He bared his arm and showed his strength, scattered the bluffing braggarts.
MSG.52: He knocked tyrants off their high horses, pulled victims out of the mud.
MSG.53: The starving poor sat down to a banquet; the callous rich were left out in the cold.
MSG.54: He embraced his chosen child, Israel; he remembered and piled on the mercies, piled them high.
MSG.55: It's exactly what he promised, beginning with Abraham and right up to now.
MSG.56: Mary stayed with Elizabeth for three months and then went back to her own home.
MSG.57: When Elizabeth was full-term in her pregnancy, she bore a son.
MSG.58: Her neighbors and relatives, seeing that God had overwhelmed her with mercy, celebrated with her.
MSG.59: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child and were calling him Zachariah after his father.
MSG.60: But his mother intervened: "No. He is to be called John."
MSG.61: "But," they said, "no one in your family is named that."
MSG.62: They used sign language to ask Zachariah what he wanted him named.
MSG.63: Asking for a tablet, Zachariah wrote, "His name is to be John." That took everyone by surprise.
MSG.64: Surprise followed surprise—Zachariah's mouth was now open, his tongue loose, and he was talking, praising God!
MSG.65: A deep, reverential fear settled over the neighborhood, and in all that Judean hill country people talked about nothing else.
MSG.66: Everyone who heard about it took it to heart, wondering, "What will become of this child? Clearly, God has his hand in this."
MSG.67: Then Zachariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied,
MSG.68: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel; he came and set his people free.
MSG.69: He set the power of salvation in the center of our lives, and in the very house of David his servant,
MSG.70: Just as he promised long ago through the preaching of his holy prophets:
MSG.71: Deliverance from our enemies and every hateful hand;
MSG.72: Mercy to our fathers, as he remembers to do what he said he'd do,
MSG.73: What he swore to our father Abraham—
MSG.74: a clean rescue from the enemy camp, So we can worship him without a care in the world,
MSG.75: made holy before him as long as we live.
MSG.76: And you, my child, "Prophet of the Highest," will go ahead of the Master to prepare his ways,
MSG.77: Present the offer of salvation to his people, the forgiveness of their sins.
MSG.78: Through the heartfelt mercies of our God, God's Sunrise will break in upon us,
MSG.79: Shining on those in the darkness, those sitting in the shadow of death, Then showing us the way, one foot at a time, down the path of peace.
MSG.80: The child grew up, healthy and spirited. He lived out in the desert until the day he made his prophetic debut in Israel.

NIV.1:
Introduction
Many have undertaken to draw up an account of the things that have been fulfilled among us,
NIV.2: just as they were handed down to us by those who from the first were eyewitnesses and servants of the word.
NIV.3: Therefore, since I myself have carefully investigated everything from the beginning, it seemed good also to me to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
NIV.4: so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.
NIV.5:
The Birth of John the Baptist Foretold
In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah; his wife Elizabeth was also a descendant of Aaron.
NIV.6: Both of them were upright in the sight of God, observing all the Lord’s commandments and regulations blamelessly.
NIV.7: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren; and they were both well along in years.
NIV.8: Once when Zechariah’s division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God,
NIV.9: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to go into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
NIV.10: And when the time for the burning of incense came, all the assembled worshipers were praying outside.
NIV.11: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
NIV.12: When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and was gripped with fear.
NIV.13: But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid, Zechariah; your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to give him the name John.
NIV.14: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice because of his birth,
NIV.15: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even from birth.
NIV.16: Many of the people of Israel will he bring back to the Lord their God.
NIV.17: And he will go on before the Lord, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous—to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
NIV.18: Zechariah asked the angel, “How can I be sure of this? I am an old man and my wife is well along in years.”
NIV.19: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to tell you this good news.
NIV.20: And now you will be silent and not able to speak until the day this happens, because you did not believe my words, which will come true at their proper time.”
NIV.21: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah and wondering why he stayed so long in the temple.
NIV.22: When he came out, he could not speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he kept making signs to them but remained unable to speak.
NIV.23: When his time of service was completed, he returned home.
NIV.24: After this his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and for five months remained in seclusion.
NIV.25: “The Lord has done this for me,” she said. “In these days he has shown his favor and taken away my disgrace among the people.”
NIV.26:
The Birth of Jesus Foretold
In the sixth month, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a town in Galilee,
NIV.27: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.
NIV.28: The angel went to her and said, “Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you.”
NIV.29: Mary was greatly troubled at his words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
NIV.30: But the angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, you have found favor with God.
NIV.31: You will be with child and give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus.
NIV.32: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David,
NIV.33: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever; his kingdom will never end.”
NIV.34: “How will this be,” Mary asked the angel, “since I am a virgin?”
NIV.35: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the holy one to be born will be called the Son of God.
NIV.36: Even Elizabeth your relative is going to have a child in her old age, and she who was said to be barren is in her sixth month.
NIV.37: For nothing is impossible with God.”
NIV.38: “I am the Lord’s servant,” Mary answered. “May it be to me as you have said.” Then the angel left her.
NIV.39:
Mary Visits Elizabeth
At that time Mary got ready and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea,
NIV.40: where she entered Zechariah’s home and greeted Elizabeth.
NIV.41: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
NIV.42: In a loud voice she exclaimed: “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you will bear!

NRSVCE.1: Since many have undertaken to set down an orderly account of the events that have been fulfilled among us,
NRSVCE.2: just as they were handed on to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word,

NRSVCE.4: so that you may know the truth concerning the things about which you have been instructed.
NRSVCE.5: In the days of King Herod of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly order of Abijah. His wife was a descendant of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
NRSVCE.6: Both of them were righteous before God, living blamelessly according to all the commandments and regulations of the Lord.
NRSVCE.7: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren, and both were getting on in years.
NRSVCE.8: Once when he was serving as priest before God and his section was on duty,
NRSVCE.9: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and offer incense.
NRSVCE.10: Now at the time of the incense offering, the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.
NRSVCE.11: Then there appeared to him an angel of the Lord, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
NRSVCE.12: When Zechariah saw him, he was terrified; and fear overwhelmed him.
NRSVCE.13: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John.
NRSVCE.14: You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
NRSVCE.15: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He must never drink wine or strong drink; even before his birth he will be filled with the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.16: He will turn many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.
NRSVCE.17: With the spirit and power of Elijah he will go before him, to turn the hearts of parents to their children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
NRSVCE.18: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know that this is so? For I am an old man, and my wife is getting on in years.”
NRSVCE.19: The angel replied, “I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
NRSVCE.20: But now, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time, you will become mute, unable to speak, until the day these things occur.”
NRSVCE.21: Meanwhile the people were waiting for Zechariah, and wondered at his delay in the sanctuary.
NRSVCE.22: When he did come out, he could not speak to them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He kept motioning to them and remained unable to speak.
NRSVCE.23: When his time of service was ended, he went to his home.
NRSVCE.24: After those days his wife Elizabeth conceived, and for five months she remained in seclusion. She said,
NRSVCE.25: “This is what the Lord has done for me when he looked favorably on me and took away the disgrace I have endured among my people.”

NRSVCE.27: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.

NET.1: Now many have undertaken to compile an account of the things that have been fulfilled among us,
NET.2: like the accounts passed on to us by those who were eyewitnesses and servants of the word from the beginning.
NET.3: So it seemed good to me as well, because I have followed all things carefully from the beginning, to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
NET.4: so that you may know for certain the things you were taught.
NET.5: During the reign of Herod king of Judea, there lived a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah, and he had a wife named Elizabeth, who was a descendant of Aaron.
NET.6: They were both righteous in the sight of God, following all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blamelessly.
NET.7: But they did not have a child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both very old.
NET.8: Now while Zechariah was serving as priest before God when his division was on duty,
NET.9: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the holy place of the Lord and burn incense.
NET.10: Now the whole crowd of people were praying outside at the hour of the incense offering.
NET.11: An angel of the Lord, standing on the right side of the altar of incense, appeared to him.
NET.12: And Zechariah, visibly shaken when he saw the angel, was seized with fear.
NET.13: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son; you will name him John.
NET.14: Joy and gladness will come to you, and many will rejoice at his birth,
NET.15: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He must never drink wine or strong drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth.
NET.16: He will turn many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.
NET.17: And he will go as forerunner before the Lord in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers back to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just, to make ready for the Lord a people prepared for him.”
NET.18: Zechariah said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is old as well.”
NET.19: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and I was sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
NET.20: And now, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time, you will be silent, unable to speak, until the day these things take place.”
NET.21: Now the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they began to wonder why he was delayed in the holy place.
NET.22: When he came out, he was not able to speak to them. They realized that he had seen a vision in the holy place, because he was making signs to them and remained unable to speak.
NET.23: When his time of service was over, he went to his home.
NET.24: After some time his wife Elizabeth became pregnant, and for five months she kept herself in seclusion. She said,
NET.25: “This is what the Lord has done for me at the time when he has been gracious to me, to take away my disgrace among people.”
NET.26: In the sixth month of Elizabeth’s pregnancy, the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a town of Galilee called Nazareth,
NET.27: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, a descendant of David, and the virgin’s name was Mary.
NET.28: The angel came to her and said, “Greetings, favored one, the Lord is with you!”
NET.29: But she was greatly troubled by his words and began to wonder about the meaning of this greeting.
NET.30: So the angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God!
NET.31: Listen: You will become pregnant and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
NET.32: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give him the throne of his father David.
NET.33: He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
NET.34: Mary said to the angel, “How will this be, since I have not had sexual relations with a man?”
NET.35: The angel replied, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore the child to be born will be holy; he will be called the Son of God.
NET.36: “And look, your relative Elizabeth has also become pregnant with a son in her old age — although she was called barren, she is now in her sixth month!
NET.37: For nothing will be impossible with God.”
NET.38: So Mary said, “Yes, I am a servant of the Lord; let this happen to me according to your word.” Then the angel departed from her.
NET.39: In those days Mary got up and went hurriedly into the hill country, to a town of Judah,
NET.40: and entered Zechariah’s house and greeted Elizabeth.
NET.41: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
NET.42: She exclaimed with a loud voice, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child in your womb!
NET.43: And who am I that the mother of my Lord should come and visit me?
NET.44: For the instant the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.
NET.45: And blessed is she who believed that what was spoken to her by the Lord would be fulfilled.”
NET.46: And Mary said, “My soul exalts the Lord,
NET.47: and my spirit has begun to rejoice in God my Savior,
NET.48: because he has looked upon the humble state of his servant. For from now on all generations will call me blessed,
NET.49: because he who is mighty has done great things for me, and holy is his name;
NET.50: from generation to generation he is merciful to those who fear him.
NET.51: He has demonstrated power with his arm; he has scattered those whose pride wells up from the sheer arrogance of their hearts.
NET.52: He has brought down the mighty from their thrones, and has lifted up those of lowly position;

NET.54: He has helped his servant Israel, remembering his mercy,
NET.55: as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to his descendants forever.”

NET.57: Now the time came for Elizabeth to have her baby, and she gave birth to a son.
NET.58: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown great mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.
